title: wtf-actor: Define WorkflowInstance actor structs and InstanceMsg enum

resolved_clarifications.question: What serialization format for journal entries?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Use serde JSON for JournalEntry, stored in sled as binary

resolved_clarifications.question: What error types needed?
resolved_clarifications.answer: StepError, SignalError defined in wtf-core/errors.rs following ADR-010

assumptions.assumption: InstanceState includes dag: WorkflowGraph from wtf-core

assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Replay logic will have wrong entry types

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL use ractor Actor trait for WorkflowInstance
THE SYSTEM SHALL use async fn for all message handlers

event_driven.trigger: WHEN MasterOrchestrator spawns WorkflowInstance via pre_start
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL load workflow definition and build DAG
THE SYSTEM SHALL execute step with journal replay logic
THE SYSTEM SHALL resolve wait_for_signal with payload

unwanted.condition: IF journal entry exists for step_id
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT re-execute the step

system_state: Storage (sled::Db) must be open and accessible

invariants: DAG has no cycles (topological order exists)
status is one of: Running, WaitingForSignal, Completed, Failed

title: wtf-actor: Implement WorkflowInstance pre_start - load workflow, build DAG, load journal

resolved_clarifications.question: How to load workflow definition from storage?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Read from sled using invocation_id key prefix 'workflow:'

resolved_clarifications.question: How to build DAG from workflow definition?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Parse workflow definition JSON, create petgraph::Graph with step nodes

assumptions.assumption: Workflow definition stored as JSON in sled under key 'workflow:{workflow_name}'

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL load workflow before executing any steps
THE SYSTEM SHALL build DAG with topological ordering

event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL execute pre_start to initialize state

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT proceed to Running state
unwanted.because: Invalid workflow could cause undefined behavior

state_changes: journal is Vec<JournalEntry> loaded from storage or empty
journal_cursor is 0 or len of loaded journal

invariants: journal.len() == journal_cursor + number of completed steps
DAG has exactly one start node (no incoming edges)
DAG has at most one end node (no outgoing edges)

files_to_read.what_to_extract: How to read from sled storage

data_integrity_failures.prevention: Validate topological sort succeeds before setting status to Running

title: wtf-actor: Implement execute_step_with_replay - core journal replay logic

resolved_clarifications.question: How to check if step already executed in journal?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Scan journal for entry with matching step_id, if output exists return cached

resolved_clarifications.question: What if journal has entry but no output (incomplete)?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Treat as not executed, execute step and overwrite entry

assumptions.assumption: Step ID corresponds to journal index

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL return cached result if step already executed
THE SYSTEM SHALL execute step if no cached result exists

event_driven.trigger: WHEN ExecuteStep is called with step_id
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL check journal for existing result first

unwanted.condition: IF step has cached result in journal
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT call execute_step_live
THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT cache the failure in journal until retries exhausted

required_inputs.constraints: Must be valid node index in DAG

state_changes: journal updated with step result if executed live
journal_cursor advanced if new entry appended

return_guarantees.guarantee: Either cached from journal or from execute_step_live

invariants: Journal entry for step_id has output iff step was executed

data_integrity_failures.prevention: Verify step_id matches before returning cached result

error_paths.given: Journal entry exists but output corrupted

Return cached output if found
Call execute_step_live and append result to journal

title: wtf-actor: Implement execute_step_live - actually execute a workflow step

resolved_clarifications.question: How to get step definition from DAG?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Use petgraph node_weights to get Step struct for step_id

resolved_clarifications.question: How to spawn activity actors?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Use Actor::spawn_linked to create ActivityActor per ADR-003

assumptions.validation_method: ADR-003 hierarchy shows ActivityActor as child

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL execute step according to DAG ordering
THE SYSTEM SHALL respect step dependencies

event_driven.trigger: WHEN step has all dependencies complete
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL execute the step

unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT execute the step

required_inputs.constraints: Must be valid node index with all dependencies satisfied

system_state: Parent steps must have outputs in journal

state_changes: Step output available for dependent steps

invariants: Step only executes after all ancestors complete
Output stored in journal for replay

tasks.what: Test successful step execution
async fn execute_step_live(&mut self, step_id: u32) -> Result<StepOutput, StepError>
Check all ancestor steps have outputs in journal
Get step definition using node_weight
Spawn ActivityActor and call ExecuteActivity
Return StepOutput from activity execution

do: Use call! macro for ActivityActor communication
do_not: Do NOT execute step if any dependency missing

title: wtf-actor: Implement handle_signal - resolve wait_for_signal journal entries

resolved_clarifications.question: How to find pending wait_for_signal in journal?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Scan journal backwards for most recent WaitForSignal entry without payload

resolved_clarifications.question: What happens if multiple wait_for_signal pending?
resolved_clarifications.answer: Resolve the most recent one (LIFO order)

assumptions.assumption: Signal name must match pending wait_for_signal name

ubiquitous: THE SYSTEM SHALL only resolve signals for pending wait_for_signal entries

event_driven.trigger: WHEN Signal message received with matching signal_name
event_driven.shall: THE SYSTEM SHALL update pending WaitForSignal entry with payload

event_driven.trigger: WHEN Signal received but no matching wait_for_signal

unwanted.condition: IF signal received for already-resolved WaitForSignal
unwanted.shall_not: THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT overwrite existing payload
unwanted.because: Signal already delivered, duplicate should be rejected

system_state: Journal must contain WaitForSignal entry for signal_name

return_guarantees.guarantee: Ok(()) if signal resolved, Err if no pending signal

invariants: WaitForSignal entry can only be resolved once

edge_cases.name: test_handle_signal_when_already_resolved
edge_cases.input: Signal sent twice with same name

execute.command: Send Signal message to workflow instance

gate_2_implementation.name: Implementation Gate
checks: [ ] Returns error if no pending signal

tasks.what: Test signal resolves pending wait
Test signal error when no pending
async fn handle_signal(&mut self, signal_name: &str, payload: Vec<u8>) -> Result<(), SignalError>
Scan journal backwards for WaitForSignal with matching name and no payload
Update WaitForSignal entry with payload
Return SignalError::NoPendingSignal if not found

failure_modes.likely_cause: Not scanning from most recent entry

do: Only resolve if payload is None
